title: [Bug]: NATS password authentication can be configured without a username
summary: NATS password settings can be supplied without a username, leaving an incomplete authentication mode to fail later.
observed: A configuration with a NATS password source but no NATS username validates successfully.
expected: Username and password authentication must require both a username and exactly one password source.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that configures a password source without a username.
impact: An incomplete authentication policy can reach runtime connection setup and produce less actionable errors.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. The fix fails incomplete authentication before connection attempts.
security: Fail-closed authentication validation keeps credential mode selection explicit and reviewable.
acceptance: NATS password sources without nats.user are rejected.
Complete username and password authentication continues to work.
Unauthenticated NATS connections continue to be supported when no auth fields are configured.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, NATS connection option tests, configuration tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update NATS authentication documentation if the user/password pairing policy is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
